analysis: Phase Category False Positive Root-Cause Analysis
summary: All previously reported Phase FPs (2 in critiques) were traced to benchmark labeling errors, not verifier imprecision.

findings.detail: Benchmark was labeled has_bug=False, but nn.LayerNorm(64) after Conv2d(3,64,3,padding=1) with input (batch,3,32,32) produces output (batch,64,32,32). LayerNorm(64) expects last dim=64 but gets 32. Verified: torch raises RuntimeError: Given normalized_shape=[64], expected input with shape [*, 64], but got input of size[1, 64, 32, 32].
findings.fix: Changed LayerNorm(64) to LayerNorm([64, 32, 32]) to normalize over channels+spatial dims, matching the description "channels-last style".

breakdown.detail: Missing AdaptiveAvgPool2d+flatten before Linear. Conv2d output (batch,64,32,32) fed directly to Linear(64,10) which expects last dim=64 but gets 32.
Missing pool+flatten. Conv2d(3,16,3,padding=1) output (batch,16,32,32) fed directly to Linear(16,10).
Missing pool+flatten. Two Conv2d outputs (batch,64,32,32) fed directly to Linear(64,10).
Identical to reshape_sequential_conv_flatten_correct. Missing pool+flatten before Linear.

additional_fp_analysis.conclusion: All 5 FPs were benchmark labeling errors (classification (c)). No verifier imprecision, encoding errors, or theory combination artifacts found. TensorGuard was correct in all cases. After correcting benchmark labels, TensorGuard achieves P=1.000 (0 FP) on the full 205-benchmark suite.

phase_theory_assessment.note: The Phase theory operates over a 2-element finite domain {TRAIN, EVAL} and is fully decidable. After benchmark correction, it achieves perfect F1=1.000 with 0 FPs, confirming that the PhasePropagator encoding is faithful to PyTorch train/eval semantics for dropout, batchnorm, instancenorm, groupnorm, and alphadropout.
